info.title: Iru Endpoint Management API
info.description:
# Welcome to the Iru Endpoint Management API Documentation

**Note:** Kandji is in the process of changing to Iru. Many URLs and notes within this documentation will continue to reference Kandji for some time.

You can find your API URL in Settings > Access. The API URL will follow the below formats.

- US - `https://SubDomain.api.kandji.io`
    
- EU - `https://SubDomain.api.eu.kandji.io`
    

For information on how to obtain an API token, please refer to the Iru docs.

[https://docs.iru.com/](https://docs.iru.com/)

#### Rate Limit

The Iru Endpoint Management API currently has an API rate limit of 10,000 requests per hour per customer.

#### Request Methods

HTTP request methods supported by the API.

| Method | Definition |
| --- | --- |
| GET | The `GET` method requests a representation of the specified resource. |
| POST | The `POST` method submits an entity to the specified resource. |
| PATCH | The `PATCH` method applies partial modifications to a resource. |
| DELETE | The `DELETE` method deletes the specified resource. |

#### Response codes

Not all response codes apply to every endpoint.

| Code | Response |
| --- | --- |
| 200 | OK |
| 201 | Created |
| 204 | No content |
|  | Typical response when sending the DELETE method. |
| 400 | Bad Request |
|  | "Command already running" - The command may already be running in a _Pending_ state waiting on the device. |
|  | "Command is not allowed for current device" - The command may not be compatible with the target device. |
|  | "JSON parse error - Expecting ',' delimiter: line 3 column 2 (char 65)" |
| 401 | Unauthorized |
|  | This error can occur if the token is incorrect, was revoked, or the token has expired. |
| 403 | Forbidden |
|  | The request was understood but cannot be authorized. |
| 404 | Not found |
|  | Unable to locate the resource in the Iru tenant. |
| 415 | Unsupported Media Type |
|  | The request contains a media type which the server or resource does not support. |
| 500 | Internal server error |
| 503 | Service unavailable |
|  | This error can occur if a file upload is still being processed via the custom apps API. |

#### Data structure

The API returns all structured responses in JSON schema format.

#### Examples

Code examples using the API can be found in the Iru Endpoint Management support [GitHub](https://github.com/kandji-inc/support/tree/main/api-tools).

get.description: This request returns audit log events from the Kandji Activity module.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: A max upper `limit` is set at 500 records returned per request. Pagination should be used using the cursor in the `next` key to request more results. Additionally, parameter queries can be added to a request to filter the results.

parameters.name: sort_by
parameters.description: Sort results by `occurred_at`, `id` either ascending (default behavior) or descending(-) order.

parameters.name: start_date
parameters.description: Filter by start date in datetime or year-month-day (2024-11-26) formats

parameters.name: end_date
parameters.description: Filter by end date in datetime or year-month-day (2024-12-06) formats

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: You can pass the next cursor as a parameter or use the URL in the next key to get the next page of results or to start from where you left off last.

get.description: This request returns the public key used to create an MDM server connection in Apple Business or Apple School Manager.

200.description: Successful response - returns the public certificate

post.description: This request will create a new ADE integration.

file.description: MDM server token file from Apple Business or Apple School Manager

post.description: This request will renew an existing ADE integration.

patch.description: This request will update the default blueprint, phone number, and email address in an existing ADE integration.

get.description: This request returns a list of configured ADE integrations.

get.summary: List devices associated to ADE token
get.description: This request returns a list of devices associated with a specified ade_token_id as well as their enrollment status.
Get a list of Automated Device Enrollment devices.

parameters.name: blueprint_id
parameters.description: Return results "containing" the specified blueprint id

parameters.name: user_id
parameters.description: "exact" match on user ID value (example: 5344c996-8823-4b37-8d6e-8515fc7c3a0a)

parameters.name: dep_account
parameters.description: The ADE token UUID

parameters.name: device_family
parameters.description: iPad, iPhone, iPod, Mac, AppleTV, or Vision

parameters.name: model
parameters.description: Return model results "containing" the specified model string. - "iPad (8th Generation)", "MacBook Air"

parameters.name: os
parameters.description: iOS, iPadOS, OSX, tvOS, or visionOS

parameters.name: profile_status
parameters.description: The automated device enrollment profile assignment status - assigned, empty, pushed, removed

parameters.name: serial_number
parameters.description: Search for a specific device by Serial Number. If partial serial number is provided in the query, all device containing the partial string will be returned.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Use the `page` parameter to page through results or to request a specific page. By default, if a page is not specified, page 1 is returned. Note: 300 device records are returned per page of results. Alternatively, the `next` and `previous` key attributes in the response can be used to request the next page of results or return to the previous page.

get.description: Get information about a specific Automated Device Enrollment device.

patch.description: Update a specific Automated Device Enrollment device's blueprint assignment, user assignment, and asset tag.

get.description: This request returns a list of blueprint records in the Iru Endpoint Management tenant.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Look up a specific Blueprint by its ID

parameters.name: id__in
parameters.description: Specify a list of Blueprint IDs to limit the results to. Multiple values may be separated by commas. There is a double underscore (__) between id and in

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Return Blueprint names "containing" the specified search string.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Number of results to return per page.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: The initial index from which to return the results.

results.name: this is a test assignment map

post.description: This request creates a new empty Blueprint or a new Blueprint from a template.

name.description: The name of the Blueprint. Must be unique.

enrollment_code.is_active.description: Enable or disable the Blueprint for manual device enrollment.

enrollment_code.code.description: The enrollment code for the Blueprint. If not provided, will be randomly generated.

type.description: The type of blueprint. Use 'map' for Assignment Map blueprints.

get.description: This request returns the manual enrollment profile (.mobileconfig file) for a specified Blueprint.

parameters.name: blueprint_id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the blueprint.

parameters.name: sso
parameters.description: Use the `sso` query parameter, set to `true`, to return a URL instead of the manual enrollment profile. This parameter should only be used for blueprints in which "Require Authentication" is configured for Manual Enrollment. The returned URL must be used to authenticate via SSO to receive an enrollment profile.

example.detail: Bad Request - Invalid blueprint ID or parameters
Unauthorized - Invalid or missing API token

templates.description:
CIS Level 1 and Level 2 Scored parameters, based on the macOS benchmark created and maintained by Center for Internet Security. More details can be found at benchmarks.cisecurity.org.

CIS ”Scored" parameters are parameters that will decrease a final CIS benchmark score if they fail audit.

CIS “Level 1” parameters are intended to be practical and prudent, provide a clear security benefit, and not inhibit the utility of the technology beyond acceptable means.

CIS "Level 2" parameters are intended for environments or use cases where security is paramount, and may negatively inhibit the utility or performance of the technology.

This template is meant to be a starting point for macOS CIS Level 1 & 2 Scored compliance. Please review, and make changes as needed. Always thoroughly test before deploying to a production environment.
templates.name: CIS Level 2 Scored

details.value: This system is reserved for authorized use only, and the use of this system may be monitored.

templates.description:
CIS Level 1 Scored parameters, based on the macOS benchmark created and maintained by Center for Internet Security. More details can be found at https://benchmarks.cisecurity.org.

CIS ”Scored" parameters are parameters that will decrease a final CIS benchmark score if they fail audit.

CIS “Level 1” parameters are intended to be practical and prudent, provide a clear security benefit, and not inhibit the utility of the technology beyond acceptable means.

This template is meant to be a starting point for macOS CIS Level 1 Scored compliance. Please review, and make changes as needed. Always thoroughly test before deploying to a production environment.
templates.name: CIS Level 1 Scored

templates.description:
Parameters found in Kandji Level II as well as advanced media and network configurations. Several built-in macOS features have been disabled. 

Important: Always review and test thoroughly before deploying. Users day to day impact will be affected. It is recommended that you review these changes with end-users before deployment.
templates.name: Kandji Level III

details.value: This system is reserved for authorized use only, and the use of this system may be monitored.

templates.description:
Security is paramount by including all that is enabled in Kandji Level III, with added parameters disabling iCloud related services, and several additional macOS functions. 


Important: Always review and test thoroughly before deploying. Users day to day impact will be affected. It is recommended that you review these changes with end-users before deployment.
templates.name: Kandji Level IV

details.value: This system is reserved for authorized use only, and the use of this system may be monitored.

templates.description:
All of the parameters enabled in Kandji Level I with additional items that include bolstered authentication, password complexity, logging, and data security.

Important: Always review and test thoroughly before deploying. Users day to day impact will be affected. It is recommended that you review these changes with end-users before deployment.
templates.name: Kandji Level II

templates.description:
A minimum level of security that is recommended for all businesses. Minimal user impact can be expected. 

Important: Always review and test thoroughly before deploying. Users day to day impact will be affected. It is recommended that you review these changes with end-users before deployment.
templates.name: Kandji Level I

post.description: This endpoint sends an MDM command to remotely turn on lost mode on iOS and iPadOS.

requestBody.description: Optional JSON payload to set lock message, phone number, and footnote on the target device

Message.description: Lock screen message to display on the device

PhoneNumber.description: Phone number to display on the lock screen

Footnote.description: Footnote text to display on the lock screen

example.Message: Hey i just did this from the enable lost mode API

post.description: This command will send a request to turn off lost mode on iOS and iPadOS.
This command will tell the target iOS or iPadOS device to play the lost mode sound.
This endpoint sends an MDM command to update the location data on iOS and iPadOS.
This endpoint sends an MDM command to clear a device passcode. Available for iPhone and iPad.

get.description: This request returns the high-level information for a specified Device ID.

delete.description: This endpoint deletes a specific device.

patch.description: This request allows you to update device information, such as the assigned blueprint, user, Asset Tag,

user.description: The user ID (UUID or deprecated integer value). Can be set to null to clear the assigned user.

asset_tag.description: The asset tag for the device. Can be set to null to clear the asset tag.

blueprint_id.description: The blueprint ID to assign to the device.

tags.description: Array of tag names to assign to the device. Can be set to [] to clear all tags.

post.description:
This endpoint sends an MDM command to erase the device.
iOS 4.0+, iPadOS 4.0+, macOS 10.7+, tvOS 10.2+

PIN.description: The six-character PIN for Find My. This value is available in macOS 10.8 and later. Note: This value will be ignored for iOS, iPadOS, and tvOS devices.

PreserveDataPlan.description: If true, preserve the data plan on an iPhone or iPad with eSIM functionality, if one exists. This value is available in iOS 11 and later. Default: true

DisallowProximitySetup.description: If true, disable Proximity Setup on the next reboot and skip the pane in Setup Assistant. This value is available in iOS 11 and later. Prior to iOS 14, don't use this option with any other option. Default: false

ReturnToService.description: (iOS 17 and later and iPadOS 17 and later and with Shared iPad) When sending the erase device command to mobile devices, use this key to enable Return to Service. Include an optional Wi-Fi payload ProfileId to allow the device to connect to a Wi-Fi network automatically after being erased. If a Wi-Fi ProfileId is not provided and the mobile device is not tethered to a Mac to share the network connection, the end-user will be required to select a Wi-Fi network to complete the setup. If sent to any macOS computer or to mobile devices on iOS 16 or iPadOS 16 and below, the RTS keys will be ignored, and only the erase device command will be issued to the device.

Enabled.description: (Required) If true, the device tries to re-enroll itself automatically after erasure. The user needs to deactivate all activation locks for this feature to work correctly.

ProfileId.description: Profile ID value associated with a Wi-Fi profile payload. This is required when the device doesn't have ethernet access.

get.description: This endpoint sends a request to get information about the commands sent to a given device ID.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: A hard upper `limit` is set at 300 device records returned per request. If more device records are expected, pagination should be used using the `limit` and `offset` parameters. Additionally, parameter queries can be added to a request to limit the results.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: The initial index from which to return the results.

post.description: This endpoint sends an MDM command to remotely lock a device.

Message.description: The lock message to display on the device.

PhoneNumber.description: The phone number to display on the device.

post.description: This endpoint sends an MDM command reinstall the Kandji Agent.
This endpoint sends an MDM command to control the Remote Management status on a Mac.

EnableRemoteDesktop.description: Enable or disable Remote Desktop on the device

post.description: This endpoint sends an MDM command to re-install the existing root MDM profile for a given device ID.
This endpoint sends an MDM command to remotely restart a device.

RebuildKernelCache.description: If `true`, the system rebuilds the kernel cache during a device restart. If `BootstrapTokenAllowedForAuthentication` is `true` in `SecurityInfoResponse.SecurityInfo`, the device requests the bootstrap token from MDM before executing this command. This value is available in macOS 11 and later. Default: false

NotifyUser.description: If `true`, notifies the user to restart the device at their convenience. Forced restart if the device is at `loginwindow` with no logged-in users. The user can dismiss the notification and ignore the request. No further notifications display unless you resend the command. This value is available in macOS 11.3 and later. Default: false

post.description: This endpoint sends an MDM command to initiate a blank push.
This endpoint sends an MDM command to set the device name.

DeviceName.description: The new device name to set.

post.description: This endpoint sends an MDM command to shutdown a device.
This endpoint sends an MDM command to start a check-in for a device, initiating the daily MDM commands and MDM logic.
This endpoint sends an MDM command to unlock a user account that was locked by the system because of too many failed password attempts.

get.description: This request gets all notes for the specified Device ID.

post.description: This request creates a note for the specified device ID.

content.description: The content of the device note.

get.description: This request retrieves a specified note (Note ID) for the specified Device ID.

delete.description: This request deletes a specified note (Note ID) for the specified Device ID.

patch.description: This request patches a specified note (Note ID) for the specified Device ID.

content.description: The updated content of the device note.

example.author: Test User
example.content: This is an example of updating a notes contents!

get.description: This request returns a list of devices in an Iru Endpoint Management tenant.

parameters.name: asset_tag
parameters.description: Filter by asset tag

parameters.name: blueprint_id
parameters.description: Return results "containing" the specified blueprint id

parameters.name: device_id
parameters.description: Filter by device ID

parameters.name: device_name
parameters.description: Filter by device name

parameters.name: filevault_enabled
parameters.description: Query for devices that either have FileVault on (true) or off (false). This parameter only applies to macOS. An empty list will be returned if no devices are found with the given parameter value.

parameters.name: mac_address
parameters.description: Search for a specific device by MAC address

parameters.name: model
parameters.description: Return model results "containing" the specified model string

parameters.name: ordering
parameters.description: The ordering parameter can be used to define how the device records are ordered in the response. Prepending a dash (-) to the parameter value will reverse the order of the returned results. Possible values are asset_tag, blueprint_id, device_id, device_name, last_check_in, model, platform, os_version, serial_number, user. Multiple values can be combined in a comma separated list.

parameters.name: os_version
parameters.description: Return all device records containing the specified OS version

parameters.name: platform
parameters.description: Return all records matching a specific platform. Possible values are Mac, iPad, iPhone, AppleTV

parameters.name: serial_number
parameters.description: Search for a specific device by Serial Number. If partial serial number is provided in the query, all device containing the partial string will be returned

parameters.name: tag_name
parameters.description: Return results for given tag name. Case sensitive

parameters.name: tag_name_in
parameters.description: Return results for given tag names separate by commas. Case sensitive

parameters.name: tag_id
parameters.description: Search for a tag by its ID. Case sensitive

parameters.name: tag_id_in
parameters.description: Return results for given tag IDs separated by commas. Case sensitive

parameters.name: user
parameters.description: Return results "containing" the user name

parameters.name: user_email
parameters.description: Return results "containing" search on email address

parameters.name: user_email_exact
parameters.description: Return results "matching" the specified email address

parameters.name: user_id
parameters.description: exact match on kandji user ID

parameters.name: user_name
parameters.description: Return results "containing" the assigned user Display Name

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: A hard upper limit is set at 300 device records returned per request. If more device records are expected, pagination should be used using the limit and offset parameters. Additionally, parameter queries can be added to a request to limit the results.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Specify the starting record to return

get.description: This request returns the device details for a specified Device ID.
This request returns lost mode details for a device.

device_id.description: The unique identifier of the device

lost_mode_status.description: The current status of Lost Mode

enabled_by.description: The user who enabled Lost Mode

lock_screen_message.description: The message displayed on the lock screen

lock_screen_phone_number.description: The phone number displayed on the lock screen

lock_screen_footnote.description: The footnote displayed on the lock screen

disable_status.description: The status of disabling Lost Mode

disabled_by.description: The user who disabled Lost Mode

last_location_status.description: The status of the last location update

last_location_status_at.description: When the last location status was updated

latitude.description: The latitude of the last known location

longitude.description: The longitude of the last known location

last_location_at.description: When the last location was recorded

sound_status.description: The status of the Lost Mode sound

sound_status_at.description: When the sound status was last updated

lost_mode.lock_screen_message: Hey i just did this from the enable lost mode API

delete.description: This endpoint can be used to send a cancelation request if Lost Mode is in an error state for a given iOS or iPadOS device.

200.description: Lost Mode cancellation request sent successfully

get.description: This request returns the device activity for a specified Device ID.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: A hard upper limit is set at 300 device records returned per request. If more device records are expected, pagination should be used using the limit and offset parameters. Additionally, parameter queries can be added to a request to limit the results.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Specify the starting record to return

get.description: This request allows you to retrieve the Activation Lock Bypass code.

user_based_albc.description: The user-based Activation Lock bypass code for when Activation Lock is enabled using a personal Apple ID and Find My

device_based_albc.description: The device-based Activation Lock bypass code for when Activation Lock is enabled by the MDM server

get.description: This request allows you to retrieve the FileVault Recovery key for a macOS device.
This request allows you to retrieve the device unlock pin for a macOS device.
This request returns the Recovery Lock password.
This endpoint makes a request to retrieve a list of custom apps from the Iru Endpoint Management library.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Optional page number. Used when results exceed pagination threshold. A hard upper limit is set at 300 device records returned per request.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Number of results to return per page. A hard upper limit is set at 300 records returned per request.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: The initial index from which to return the results. Used for offset-based pagination.

post.description: This request allows you to create a custom app in the Iru Endpoint Management library.

name.description: The name for this Custom App

file_key.description: The S3 key from the Upload Custom App endpoint used to upload the custom app file.

audit_script.description: Optional audit script for the custom app.

unzip_location.description: Optional unzip location for the custom app.

active.description: Whether the custom app is active.

get.description: This endpoint retrieves details about a specific custom app from the Kandji library.

delete.description: This endpoint sends a request to delete a specific custom app from the Kandji library.

patch.description: This request allows you to update a custom app in the Iru Endpoint Management library.

name.description: The name for this Custom App

file_key.description: The S3 key used to upload the file

audit_script.description: Optional audit script for the custom app.

unzip_location.description: Optional unzip location for the custom app.

active.description: Whether the custom app is active.

post.description: This request retrieves the S3 upload details need for uploading the app to Amazon S3.

requestBody.description: JSON payload containing the name of the file being uploaded

name.description: Required - name of file being uploaded

post.description: Request export of a category.

requestBody.description: JSON payload containing the export request configuration

blueprint_ids.description: List of one or more comma separate blueprint IDs.

category.description: Only one category per export request.

device_families.description: List of one or more comma separated string values for device families.

filter.description: JSON schema object containing one or more key value pairs. For detailed information on filters, see the Filters section at the beginning of the Visibility API endpoints in this doc.

sort_by.description: Sort results by the name of a given response body key in either ascending (default behavior) or descending(`-`) order.

get.description: Get an export request's status.
This request returns the total record count for the specified Prism category.

parameters.name: category
parameters.description:
Return the count of records for the specified category. 
If a category contains spaces substitute the spaces for underscores ("_") when using the API query.

Examples:
apps
device_information
kernel_extensions
system_extensions

get.description: This request returns Iru Endpoint Management tenant licensing and utilization information.
This request returns configured tags.

parameters.name: search
parameters.description: Return resultes containing a given tag search string.

post.description: This request creates a tag.

name.description: The name of the tag to create.

patch.description: This request updates a tag name.

requestBody.description: JSON payload containing the tag update information

name.description: The new name for the tag

get.description: Get threat details.

parameters.name: classification
parameters.description: Return all records matching a specified classification. The following classification options are available: `malware` and `pup`. Leave this parameter empty to return all classification types. Example: `malware`

parameters.name: date_range
parameters.description: Return all records within a specified number of days. Any positive number of days may be specified. Examples: `7`, `30`, `60`, `90`, `180`, or `365`. Example: `7`

parameters.name: device_id
parameters.description: Search for a specific device by the device id (uuid). Example: `15fcec08-xxxx-xxxx-xxxx-7c2f950910eb`

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Return all records matching a specified status. The following status options are available: `quarantined`, `not_quarantined`, or `released`. Leave this parameter empty to return all status types. Example: `quarantined`

parameters.name: sort_by
parameters.description: Results can be sorted with the following options: `threat_name`, `classification`, `device_name`, `process_name`, `process_owner`, `detection_date`, `status`. Prepending a dash (-) to the parameter value will reverse the order of the returned results. Example: `status` or `-device_name`

parameters.name: term
parameters.description: Search term to filter threat results. The response will include anything matching the following fields: `device_name`, `file_hash`, and `file_path`. Example: `Chrome`

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: A hard upper `limit` is set at 1000 records returned per request. If more records are expected, pagination should be used using the `limit` and `offset` parameters. Example: `1000`

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Specify the starting record to return. Example: `1`

get.description: Get Behavioral Detections.

parameters.name: threat_id
parameters.description: Filter by a specific threat ID. Example: `Kandji_BD_0096`

parameters.name: classification
parameters.description: Filter by threat classification. Example: `malicious`

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Filter by threat status. Example: `blocked`

parameters.name: date_range
parameters.description: Return all records within a specified number of days. Example: `30`

parameters.name: detection_date_from
parameters.description: Start date for detection date range filter. Example: `2024-01-01`

parameters.name: detection_date_to
parameters.description: End date for detection date range filter. Example: `2024-01-31`

parameters.name: device_id
parameters.description: Search for a specific device by the device id (uuid). Example: `123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-426614174000`

parameters.name: malware_family
parameters.description: Filter by malware family. Example: `TrickBot`

parameters.name: parent_process_name
parameters.description: Filter by parent process. Example: `bash`

parameters.name: target_process_name
parameters.description: Filter by target process. Example: `python`

parameters.name: informational_tags
parameters.description: Filter by tags. Example: `exploit,privilege_escalation`

parameters.name: term
parameters.description: Search term to filter threat results. Device name, file hash, image path. Example: `device_name`

parameters.name: sort_by
parameters.description: Detections can be sorted by any of the following keys. Prepending a dash (-) to the parameter value will reverse the order. Example: `status` or `-status`

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: A hard upper limit is set at 1000 records returned per request. If more records are expected, pagination should be used using the limit and offset parameters. Example: `100`

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Specify the starting record to return. Example: `0`

results.description: Detects removal of Little Snitch by unloading the Little Snitch Launch Daemon.

get.description: Retrieve a list of all vulnerability detections across the device fleet.

parameters.name: after
parameters.description: Cursor token for pagination. Example: `eyJpZCI6MTIzNDU2Nzg5fQ==`

parameters.name: size
parameters.description: A hard upper `limit` is set at 300 records returned per request. If more records are expected, pagination should be used using the URL value returned in the `next` attribute. Additionally, filters can be added to a request to limit the results. Example: `300`

parameters.name: filter
parameters.description:
Filter on any key attribute within the response.

Available fields: `device_id`, `device_name`, `device_serial_number`, `device_model`, `device_os_version`, `blueprint_id`, `blueprint_name`, `name`, `path`, `version`, `bundle_id`, `cve_id`, `cve_description`, `cve_link`, `cvss_score`, `cvss_severity`, `detection_datetime`, `cve_published_at`, `cve_modified_at`.

Example: `{"cve_id":{"in":["CVE-2024-24795"]}}`

results.cve_description: description of the cve from nvd
results.name: Example

get.description: Retrieve a list of all vulnerabilities grouped by CVE.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: The page number of the response. Example: `1`

parameters.name: size
parameters.description: A hard upper `limit` is set at 50 records returned per request. If more records are expected, pagination should be used using the URL value returned in the `next` attribute. Additionally, filters can be added to a request to limit the results. Example: `50`

parameters.name: sort_by
parameters.description: Field to sort by. Available options: `age`, `cve_id`, `cvss_score`, `device_count`, `known_exploit`, `software` (software name), `severity`, `status`. Example: `cve_id`

parameters.name: filter
parameters.description:
Filter results. Similar to prism filters.

Filterable columns: `cve_id`, `software`, `severity`, `first_detection_date`, `status`.

Example: `{"cve_id":{"in":["CVE-2024-24795"]}}`

get.description: This request returns information about a specific blueprint based on blueprint ID.

parameters.name: blueprint_id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the blueprint.

delete.description: This is a HIGHLY destructive action - deleting a Blueprint will un-manage ALL devices assigned to the Blueprint.

parameters.name: blueprint_id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the blueprint.

patch.description: This request allows updating of the name, icon, icon color, description, enrollment code, and active status on an existing blueprint.

parameters.name: blueprint_id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the blueprint.

name.description: Update the name of the Blueprint

color.description: Update the color of the Blueprint icon. A list of possible color codes can be found at https://github.com/kandji-inc/support/wiki/Blueprint-API---Color-Codes. Color will be ignored for the map type.

description.description: Update the description of the Blueprint

enrollment_code.code.description: Update the enrollment code of the Blueprint

enrollment_code.is_active.description: Disable the Blueprint for manual device enrollment from the enrollment portal

icon.description: Update the Blueprint icon. A list of possible icon codes can be found at https://github.com/kandji-inc/support/wiki/Blueprint-API-Icon-Codes. Icon will be ignored for the map type.

post.description: This endpoint sends an MDM command to delete a local user account on macOS and Shared iPad.

parameters.name: device_id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the device.

DeleteAllUsers.description: Delete all users on the device

UserName.description: Username of the user to delete

get.description: This endpoint makes a request to retrieve a list of users from user directory integrations.

parameters.name: email
parameters.description: Returns users with email addresses containing the provided string. Example: `test_user_01@accuhive.io`

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Search for a user matching the provided UUID value. Example: `69c009ca-1f78-4bdf-bb93-08d6d39041db`

parameters.name: integration_id
parameters.description: Search for an integration matching the provided UUID value. Example: `f7461096-4ef9-43aa-88e9-ca1967ba0b38`

parameters.name: archived
parameters.description: Return only users that are either archived (`true`) or not archived (`false`). Archived users are users that appear in the Users module under the Archived tab. Example: `false`

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Cursor for the next or previous page of results. Can also store the URL in the `next` and `previous` fields in the response. Example: `eyJpZCI6MTIzNDU2Nzg5fQ==`

get.description: This request returns a specific user.

parameters.name: user_id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the user directory integration user.

delete.description: This request deletes a user directory integration user.

parameters.name: user_id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the user directory integration user.

400.description: User still assigned to one or more devices

detail.example: User still assigned to one or more devices.

post.description: This endpoint allows assigning a library item to a specific blueprint (classic and maps).

parameters.name: blueprint_id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the blueprint.

library_item_id.description: The unique identifier of the library item to assign. Example: `12345678-1234-1234-1234-123456789012`

assignment_node_id.description: The assignment node ID for maps. Not required for classic blueprints or maps without conditional logic. Example: `node-123`

get.description: This API endpoint retrieves a list of library items associated with a specific blueprint (classic and maps).

parameters.name: blueprint_id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the blueprint.

count.description: The total count of library items

next.description: The URL for the next page of results, if available. If not available will value will be null

previous.description: The URL for the previous page of results, if available. If not available will value will be null

results.description: An array containing library item objects

id.description: The ID of the library item

name.description: The name of the library item

get.description: Retrieve information about a CVE.

parameters.name: cve_id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the vulnerability. Example: `CVE-2024-12345`

example.description: description of the cve from nvd

get.description: Retrieve a list of devices impacted by a specified cve_id vulnerability for a tenants fleet.

parameters.name: cve_id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the vulnerability. Example: `CVE-2024-12345`

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: The page number of the response. Example: `1`

parameters.name: size
parameters.description: A hard upper `limit` is set at 50 records returned per request. If more records are expected, pagination should be used using the URL value returned in the `next` attribute. Additionally, filters can be added to a request to limit the results. Example: `50`

parameters.name: sort_by
parameters.description: Field to sort by. Available options: `name` (device name), `detection_datetime`. Example: `software_name`

parameters.name: filter
parameters.description:
Filter results.

Filterable columns: `name`, `detection_datetime`.

Example: `{"detection_datetime":{"gte":"2025-05-23T17:11:31.816587Z"}}`

get.description: This endpoint makes a request to retrieve a list of software impacted by a specified cve_id vulnerability for a tenant's fleet.

parameters.name: cve_id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the vulnerability. Example: `CVE-2024-12345`

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: The page number of the response. Example: `1`

parameters.name: size
parameters.description: A hard upper `limit` is set at 50 records returned per request. If more records are expected, pagination should be used using the URL value returned in the `next` attribute. Additionally, filters can be added to a request to limit the results. Example: `50`

parameters.name: sort_by
parameters.description: Field to sort by. Available options: `software` (software name), `detection_datetime`. Example: `software`

parameters.name: filter
parameters.description:
Filter results. Similar to prism filters.

Filterable columns: `blueprint_id`, `detection_datetime`.

Example: `{"detection_datetime":{"gte":"2025-05-23T17:11:31.816587Z"}}`

post.description: This endpoint sends a daily check-in command to a device.
